founder.label: Meet the Founder
founder.intro: After more than five years of tutoring students from KS3 to A Level, Lukas founded Yuli around a simple belief: students remember what they understand, not what they memorise. That principle shapes every lesson at Yuli. We break subjects down to their logic so students enter exams with real confidence, not just recall.

intro.p1: Yuli is a tutoring company for GCSE and A-Level students, covering subjects from Mathematics to English Literature, from KS3 through to A-Level, against every major UK exam board.
intro.p2: It was made around one belief: students remember what they understand, not what they memorise. Most tutoring optimises for the next test, drilling answers until they stick. That rarely lasts, once the wording changes or the topic moves on, memorised answers fall apart. Understanding doesn't. Every Yuli lesson is built around making a topic actually make sense, not just recallable for a few weeks.

approach.lead: The method stays consistent, even though every student is different.

card1.title: Clear explanations
card1.desc: Every topic is broken down to its underlying logic first, rather than handed over as a shortcut to memorise.

card2.title: Adapted to the student
card2.desc: No fixed script. Lessons adapt to how each student thinks and learns, not the other way round.

card3.title: Real understanding, real confidence
card3.desc: Ideas are connected to real examples so they genuinely make sense, building confidence that holds up under pressure.

card4.title: Applied in exams
card4.desc: Understanding is tested against real past-paper questions, so it holds up on exam day, not just in conversation.

founder.p1: Yuli was founded by Lukas Jovaisa, who's currently studying Mathematics at university. He's been tutoring for 5+ years, working with students from KS3 through to A Level, and started Yuli because he kept seeing the same gap: students taught to pass exams, not to understand them.
founder.p2: His approach comes from a simple belief, first found in language learning: understanding beats memorising, every time. He adapts to how each student thinks rather than sticking to one method, and goes outside the exam spec whenever it helps something click.
founder.p3: He still tutors while he studies, because he wants every student to reach the same moment: “wait, I can actually do this.”

going.h2: Growing without losing what makes it work
going.p1: Yuli is still young, and growing carefully matters more than growing fast. The plan is to bring in more tutors who share the same belief, that understanding lasts and memorising doesn't, so more students can learn this way without losing the personal attention that makes it work.
going.p2: Longer term, Lukas would like Yuli to move closer to the free, logic-first teaching he first found in resources like Language Transfer: making genuine understanding a little more accessible, not just for those who can pay for it.

cta.p: Explore the subjects Yuli covers, see pricing, or get in touch with any questions.

common.examBoardsNote: Yuli is not affiliated with or endorsed by AQA, Edexcel, OCR, WJEC/Eduqas, or CCEA.

howWeTeach.title: How We Teach
howWeTeach.subtitle: A personalised approach that builds confidence, true understanding, and results that last.

step1.title: 1. Diagnostic first
step1.desc: I begin with the goals parents and students bring to me, whether it is passing an exam, improving confidence, or filling gaps. Everything I do is adapted to the student’s needs.

step2.title: 2. Guided teaching
step2.desc: When I notice missing knowledge, I go back and rebuild the foundations until the student truly understands. No skipping and no rushing, just clarity.

step4.title: 4. Perform under pressure
step4.desc: I know what it’s like to worry about being judged. My students never have to feel embarrassed about not knowing something. With patience and practice, I help them build confidence for exams and understanding that really lasts.

benefit1.title: Customised exam papers
benefit1.desc: Practice papers tailored to your exam board and current gaps, with mark schemes and worked solutions.

benefit2.title: Parent feedback every lesson
benefit2.desc: A short summary after each session—what we covered, next steps, and anything to watch before the next lesson.

benefit3.title: Progress tracker & next steps
benefit3.desc: Clear milestones from the diagnostic plan, weekly targets, and visible progress over time.

onlineSessions.a1: Sessions are currently run on Microsoft Teams. I share my screen and use digital tools to explain topics clearly, step by step, while students work through problems with me in real time.
onlineSessions.a2: We are also developing our own interactive online whiteboard, which is in beta testing. Once ready, it will make lessons even more engaging and give students an easy way to practice and save their work in one place.

discounts.q: Can I get discounts on sessions?
discounts.a: Yes! We offer discounted rates for students who qualify based on household income. We believe quality education should be accessible to all students regardless of financial circumstances.

homework.q: Do you provide homework or practice after lessons?
homework.a: Yes. I often set short, focused practice tasks after lessons. These help students reinforce what we covered and prepare for the next session.

hero.title: GCSE Mathematics
hero.tagline: Clarity, confidence, and exam results that last.
hero.subtext: From £20/hr · All UK exam boards covered

gcseMaths.hwtStep3Desc: Every student learns differently. I find out what motivates them, whether it is cars, football, or music, and use that to make lessons engaging. Maths becomes relevant, understandable, and easier to remember.

testimonial1.quote: “He’s not one of those tutors who make you wish the lesson was over from the first minute. He keeps you engaged at all times, is friendly and patient, knows how to turn your weak areas into strengths, and supports you every step of the way.”

testimonial2.quote: “I usually hate maths, but I actually enjoyed the sessions with Lukas. He explained things in a way that was easy to understand and made me feel comfortable. The one-to-one classes helped a lot and weren’t stressful at all.”

testimonial3.quote: “Lukas has been brilliant with Yahya. Maths has always been a subject he struggled with, but Lukas’s easy-going approach and clear explanations made the lessons engaging and effective. Yahya felt comfortable and supported, and I could see his confidence grow.”

faqExamBoards.q: What exam boards do you cover?
faqExamBoards.a: We cover all major GCSE Mathematics exam boards including AQA, Edexcel, OCR, WJEC/Eduqas, and CCEA. Each board has slightly different approaches, and we tailor our teaching to match your specific syllabus.

faqTier.q: Do you teach both Foundation and Higher tier?
faqTier.a: Yes, we teach both Foundation and Higher tier GCSE Mathematics. We'll assess your current level and work towards your target grade, whether that's securing a solid pass or aiming for the top grades.

faqPrice.q: How much do GCSE Mathematics sessions cost?
faqPrice.a: GCSE Mathematics sessions are £20 per hour for one to one and £10 per hour for group sessions. GCSE sessions are £20 per hour for one to one and £10 per hour for group sessions. If a discount is applied the price becomes £12 per hour for one to one and £6 per hour for group sessions.
